articles.title: Reliability and clinical utility of paediatric ankle fracture classifications : a systematic review.
articles.authors: Teo LS, Xiao L, Yeo BKK, Rayapati R, Lim AKS, Hoipo Hui J, et al.
articles.outcomes: Inter- and intraobserver reliability of classification systems; predictive value for premature physeal closure and angular deformity
articles.take_home: The Dias-Tachdjian classification system was most reliable for doctors to use consistently when assessing children's ankle fractures, whilst the Salter-Harris system was most commonly used and best at predicting long-term complications. Using both classification types together may help doctors plan treatment more accurately.

articles.title: Long-term Outcomes After First Metatarsophalangeal Joint Arthrodesis: With Focus on Degenerative Changes on Adjacent Joints.
articles.authors: Recheis S, Mattausch D, Kuehn N, Kindermann H, Ortmaier R, Bischofreiter M, et al.
articles.population: Patients who underwent isolated first metatarsophalangeal joint arthrodesis
articles.sample_size: 77 patients (45 female, 32 male) followed up at mean 106.9 months
articles.outcomes: Radiographic osteoarthritis progression in adjacent joints using Kellgren-Lawrence classification; clinical outcomes measured by AOFAS HMI score, FAOS, FFI, and patient satisfaction questionnaire
articles.take_home: First metatarsophalangeal joint fusion provides excellent long-term satisfaction and function, with over 90% of patients reporting good or excellent outcomes. Although wear and tear does develop in nearby joints over time, particularly at the big toe joint, this does not affect how well patients feel their foot works.

articles.title: Multiplanar Stability After Syndesmotic Screw Fixation vs Deltoid Ligament Repair in a Cadaveric Weber B/SER4b Ankle Fracture Model With Fibular Plate Fixation.
articles.authors: Haanaes EKO, Dalen AF, Gregersen MG, Skrede A, Molund M
articles.population: Fresh frozen human cadaveric ankle specimens
articles.outcomes: Talar shift (lateral translation), valgus tilt, and internal and external rotation stability under 45-N axial load
articles.take_home: Both syndesmotic screw fixation and deltoid ligament repair restored the ankle's ability to resist sideways shift when combined with fibular plate fixation, but deltoid ligament repair was better at restoring overall stability including rotational and tilting movements.

articles.title: Patient acceptable symptom state for European Foot & Ankle Society Score After Hallux Valgus Surgery.
articles.authors: Lee HJ, Yew KSA, Yeo W, Mohamed Buhary KS, Toh RX, Li Z, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study using prospectively collected data
articles.sample_size: 361 patients (324 female, 37 male) followed for 2 years
articles.outcomes: Patient acceptable symptom state (PASS) thresholds for European Foot & Ankle Society Score and American Orthopaedic Foot & Ankle Society Score at 6 months and 2 years after surgery
articles.take_home: The study identified specific score thresholds that represent acceptable symptom improvement for patients after bunion surgery, with EFAS scores of 20.5 at 6 months and 23.5 at 2 years. These thresholds can help clinicians and researchers interpret whether patients have achieved meaningful improvement after bunion surgery.

articles.title: EFAS Score - validation of the Norwegien version by the Score Committee of the European Foot and Ankle Society (EFAS).
articles.authors: Richter M, Agren PH, Besse JL, Coester M, Kofoed H, Maffulli N, et al.
articles.population: Patients with a wide variety of foot and ankle pathologies undergoing surgery
articles.outcomes: Internal consistency (Cronbach's Alpha 0.86), Standard Error of Measurement (0.31), responsiveness (effect size 1.05), and improvement in EFAS score at minimum 3 months follow-up
articles.take_home: The Norwegian version of the EFAS Score, a questionnaire measuring foot and ankle outcomes after surgery, has been successfully validated and works reliably. 77% of patients showed improvement on their EFAS score at follow-up, indicating the measure effectively detects changes in patients' conditions.
